text.title: Static Application Security Testing (SAST) in CI/CD Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: First week of SAST: every PR red with 50 LOW / INFO findings; team negotiates a `--no-verify` exemption; gate dead within a sprint.
antipattern.root-cause: Config copy-pasted from a docs page that set `--severity WARNING`; nobody narrowed before rollout.
antipattern.fix: Narrow `severity_block` to ERROR / CRITICAL+HIGH only. Move WARNING / INFO to `severity_inform`.

antipattern.symptom: Monday morning every PR fails on the same new rule; Semgrep registry shipped a 'too-aggressive' rule overnight.
antipattern.root-cause: Workflow used Semgrep CI without pinning the registry tag or scanner image version.
antipattern.fix: Pin scanner version + use a pinned rule-pack tag / `.semgrepignore` for noisy rules.

antipattern.symptom: First SAST run on a 5-year codebase produces 2,300 findings; team decides 'we will get to it later'; SAST gate disabled.
antipattern.root-cause: Baseline step skipped; default fail-on-existing applied.
antipattern.fix: Add baseline (--baseline-ref or CodeQL alert state). Gate only on new findings in the PR diff. Address technical security debt separately.

antipattern.symptom: Findings exist but are buried in 4-week-old CI logs; triage is impossible; same issues re-appear in subsequent PRs.
antipattern.fix: Add `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3` step with `if: always()`. Verify findings appear in Security tab.
